description: Create comprehensive evidence summary figure with PTS1 conservation, similarity analysis, GO annotations, and verdict
code:
import matplotlib
matplotlib.use('Agg')
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np

# Create a comprehensive evidence summary figure
fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 2, figsize=(16, 12))

# Panel A: PTS1 conservation across species
ax1 = axes[0, 0]
species = [
    'D. melanogaster', 'D. simulans', 'D. suzukii', 
    'D. kikkawai', 'D. pseudoobscura', 'D. mauritiana',
    'D. busckii', 'D. hydei', 'D. albomicans',
    'H. sapiens\n(HADHA)', 'M. musculus\n(Hadha)', 
    'H. sapiens\n(EHHADH)', 'S. cerevisiae\n(FOX2)'
]
c_terms = ['SKL', 'SKL', 'SKL', 'SKL', 'SKL', 'SKL', 'SKL', 'AKL', 'SKL', 
           'FYQ', 'FYQ', 'SKL', 'SKL']
colors = ['#2ecc71' if ct in ['SKL', 'AKL'] else '#e74c3c' for ct in c_terms]
bars = ax1.barh(range(len(species)), [1]*len(species), color=colors, edgecolor='white', height=0.7)
for i, (sp, ct) in enumerate(zip(species, c_terms)):
    ax1.text(0.5, i, ct, ha='center', va='center', fontweight='bold', fontsize=11, color='white')
ax1.set_yticks(range(len(species)))
ax1.set_yticklabels(species, fontsize=9)
ax1.set_xlim(0, 1)
ax1.set_xticks([])
ax1.set_title('A. C-terminal Tripeptide (PTS1 Signal)', fontweight='bold')
ax1.axhline(y=8.5, color='black', linewidth=2, linestyle='-')
ax1.text(0.05, 12.3, 'Green = PTS1 present', fontsize=8, color='#2ecc71', fontweight='bold')
ax1.text(0.05, 11.8, 'Red = No PTS1', fontsize=8, color='#e74c3c', fontweight='bold')
ax1.invert_yaxis()

# Panel B: K-mer similarity
ax2 = axes[0, 1]
comparisons = ['Mtpalpha\nvs HADHA\n(mito)', 'Mtpalpha\nvs EHHADH\n(perox)', 'HADHA\nvs EHHADH']
kmer3 = [0.220, 0.128, 0.110]
kmer5 = [0.062, 0.009, 0.007]
x = np.arange(len(comparisons))
width = 0.35
bars1 = ax2.bar(x - width/2, kmer3, width, label='3-mer Jaccard', color='#3498db')
bars2 = ax2.bar(x + width/2, kmer5, width, label='5-mer Jaccard', color='#e67e22')
ax2.set_ylabel('Jaccard Similarity')
ax2.set_title('B. Sequence Similarity (k-mer)', fontweight='bold')
ax2.set_xticks(x)
ax2.set_xticklabels(comparisons, fontsize=9)
ax2.legend()
for bar_group in [bars1, bars2]:
    for bar in bar_group:
        height = bar.get_height()
        ax2.text(bar.get_x() + bar.get_width()/2., height + 0.003,
                f'{height:.3f}', ha='center', va='bottom', fontsize=9)

# Panel C: GO annotation evidence table
ax3 = axes[1, 0]
ax3.axis('off')
table_data = [
    ['GO:0005739\n(mitochondrion)', 'HDA', 'PMID:16212416', 'Mitochondrial\nproteomics'],
    ['GO:0005739\n(mitochondrion)', 'HDA', 'PMID:19317464', 'LOPIT spatial\nproteomics'],
    ['GO:0005739\n(mitochondrion)', 'ISM', 'PMID:22758915', 'Sequence model\nprediction'],
    ['GO:0005777\n(peroxisome)', 'IDA', 'PMID:22758915', 'Direct assay\n(5 confirmed)'],
    ['GO:0005777\n(peroxisome)', 'ISS', 'PMID:22758915', 'From EHHADH\n(Q08426)'],
    ['GO:0005777\n(peroxisome)', 'ISM', 'PMID:22758915', 'PTS1 prediction'],
    ['GO:0016507\n(mito FA β-ox)', 'IEA', 'InterPro', 'Domain match'],
]
col_labels = ['GO Term', 'Evidence', 'Reference', 'Basis']
table = ax3.table(cellText=table_data, colLabels=col_labels, loc='center',
                  cellLoc='center', colWidths=[0.3, 0.15, 0.25, 0.3])
table.auto_set_font_size(False)
table.set_fontsize(9)
table.scale(1, 1.8)
# Color code rows
for i in range(len(table_data)):
    row = i + 1
    if 'mitochondri' in table_data[i][0].lower():
        for j in range(4):
            table[row, j].set_facecolor('#AED6F1')
    else:
        for j in range(4):
            table[row, j].set_facecolor('#ABEBC6')
for j in range(4):
    table[0, j].set_facecolor('#D5D8DC')
    table[0, j].set_text_props(fontweight='bold')
ax3.set_title('C. GO Cellular Component Annotations for Q8IPE8', fontweight='bold', pad=20)

# Panel D: Verdict summary
ax4 = axes[1, 1]
ax4.axis('off')
verdict_text = """VERDICT: PARTIALLY SUPPORTED

The GO:0005777 (peroxisome) annotation with IDA 
evidence is supported by:
• Canonical PTS1 signal (SKL) conserved in all 
  Drosophila species examined
• IDA evidence from PMID:22758915
• ISS transfer from peroxisomal EHHADH (Q08426)

However, Mtpalpha is primarily mitochondrial:
• PANTHER: "Mitochondrial" subfamily (PTHR43612:SF3)
• Domain IPR012803: Fa_ox_alpha_mit (mito-specific)
• HDA evidence from 2 mitochondrial proteomics studies
• KO phenotype = mitochondrial β-oxidation deficiency
• Vertebrate orthologs are exclusively mitochondrial
• Drosophila has separate peroxisomal enzyme (Mfe2)

RECOMMENDATION: Retain GO:0005777 annotation.
The protein likely has genuine dual localization 
(primary: mitochondrion, secondary: peroxisome).
"""
ax4.text(0.05, 0.95, verdict_text, transform=ax4.transAxes,
         fontsize=10, verticalalignment='top', fontfamily='monospace',
         bbox=dict(boxstyle='round', facecolor='lightyellow', alpha=0.8))
ax4.set_title('D. Curation Assessment', fontweight='bold')

plt.suptitle('Mtpalpha (Q8IPE8) Peroxisome Localization Evidence Assessment', 
             fontsize=14, fontweight='bold', y=1.02)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('evidence_summary.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.show()
print("Evidence summary figure saved")
